title: Inside api-auth — components & their dependencies
subtitle: box = component · horizontal arrow = uses / calls (dependency, not time) · for execution order see the Dynamic diagram

cards: +Echo Router + Middleware|[go-common]|routing · auth/JWT middleware · binds api/* to controllers

layers.l: Other endpoint controllers (peers — each invoked directly by the Router)
layers.note: ↑ peers: they don't call each other — the Router invokes each controller directly; all share the modules & clients below.

cards: +Peer API Clients|[REST]|→ credential · challenge · oauth2 · organization · access · policy · audit

downstreamLabel: Downstream · other containers / external (context — each has its own L3)
rels: Login Controller → Peer API Clients → api-credential (verify) · → MFA Orchestrator (when MFA on) · → Token/JWT Module (issue)
Token/JWT Module → Backend Clients → backend-auth / api-oauth2 (sign — key never local)
Session & Cookie Mgr → Backend Clients → backend-auth (session in KeyDB)
note: api-auth holds no Mongo and no JWT key — all persistence/signing goes through the backends.
refs: downstream backend-auth (L3) · api-oauth2 (L3) · api-challenge — appears in Dynamic: Login + MFA — zoom out: Container view Auth & Tokens (L2)
caption: Identity Platform · Component view · api-auth (C4 L3) — dependencies (static). Execution order → Dynamic: Login + MFA.
